{"problem_standardized_math":"Show that\n\\[\n\\det(I+xy^T)=1+y^Tx,\n\\]\nwhere $x$ and $y$ are $n$-vectors. Hint: Assuming that $x\\neq 0$, choose vectors $w_1,w_2,\\ldots,w_{n-1}$ such that\n\\[\nQ=[x,w_1,w_2,\\ldots,w_{n-1}]\n\\]\nis nonsingular and $x=Qe_1$, where $e_1=(1,0,0,\\ldots,0)^T$. If\n\\[\ny^TQ=(z_1,z_2,\\ldots,z_n),\n\\]\nthen\n\\[\nz_1=y^TQe_1=y^TQ(Q^{-1}x)=y^Tx,\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\det(I+xy^T)=\\det\\bigl(Q^{-1}(I+xy^T)Q\\bigr)=\\det(I+e_1y^TQ).\n\\]"}
